hero.title: RobôCIn
hero.subtitle: A research and development team from the Federal University of Pernambuco, Brazil, specializing in robotics and artificial intelligence.

partners.title: Partnerships
partners.description: Connect your company to RobôCIn's innovation and expertise to develop intelligent solutions that shape the future of technology.
partners.caption: Who has already trusted our work:

categoriesPage.title: Competition Categories
categoriesPage.description: RobôCIn competes across four distinct robotics leagues, each presenting unique engineering and AI challenges. Explore each category to learn about our robots, approach, and competition history.

ssl.name: Small Size League (SSL)
ssl.description: The SSL is one of the oldest football leagues in RoboCup. It is focused on intelligent coordination and control of multiple agents in a highly dynamic environment. Teams compete in football matches with up to 11 robots, relying on computer vision and centralized strategy software to outmaneuver opponents in real time.
bullets: Robots built by the teams themselves, featuring kicking, passing, and dribbling systems, and limited to 180mm in diameter
Off-field software fuses overhead vision data with agent telemetry to autonomously decide the best play for each robot
Real football strategies are applied to handle ball speeds of up to 6.5 m/s

sim2d.name: 2D Simulation
sim2d.description: In the 2D Simulation league, multi-agent AI systems coach virtual soccer teams inside a physics simulator. With no hardware constraints, teams push the limits of strategy, coordination, and learning algorithms.
bullets: Fully software-based: 11 autonomous agents vs. 11 in a shared 2D environment
Focus areas include multi-agent reinforcement learning and emergent team tactics
Competitions run on identical hardware, making algorithmic quality the sole differentiator

drone.name: Drone
drone.description:
In the Drone category, the team focuses on developing autonomous unmanned aerial vehicles capable of navigating challenging environments, performing aerial manipulation tasks, visual detection, mapping, and precision landings.
Among the present challenges, the construction of drones capable of localizing themselves in GPS-denied environments stands out, along with aerial manipulation, package delivery, and target recognition.

Technical focus: Development of embedded software capable of performing sensor fusion, deep learning-based visual detection, visual-inertial odometry for indoor navigation, and the employment of control algorithms.
bullets: Onboard processing only — no external computing allowed during flight
Tasks include obstacle avoidance, target recognition, and precision landing
Tight integration of computer vision, control theory, and embedded software

humanoid.name: Humanoid
humanoid.description: Humanoid robots must walk, balance, and play soccer on a small field, showcasing advances in bipedal locomotion, real-time vision, and autonomous decision-making under physical constraints.
bullets: Full bipedal locomotion with dynamic balance and fall recovery
Onboard vision pipeline for ball and goal detection without external sensors
Motion planning adapts in real time to field conditions and game state

history.title: Our Journey
history.subtitle: Over one decade advancing Brazilian robotics.

milestones.title: Foundation
milestones.description: RobôCIn is founded by students and professors at UFPE.

milestones.title: First RoboCup
milestones.description: The group participates in its first RoboCup, one of the largest international robotics competitions.

milestones.title: Champions (LARC)
milestones.description: RobôCIn was champion in the 2D Simulation and Very Small Size Soccer (VSS) categories at the Latin American Robotics Competition.

milestones.title: Division B Champions (RoboCup)
milestones.description: RobôCIn becomes champion in RoboCup Division B in the Small Size League (SSL) category.

milestones.title: Back-to-Back Division B Champions (RoboCup)
milestones.description: The group becomes two-time champion of RoboCup Division B in the SSL category.

milestones.title: Division A Debut (RoboCup), Champions (FIRA)
milestones.description: Advances to Division A, reaching 4th place in the SSL category. The group also becomes champion in the drone category at another competition.

milestones.title: Advancing in Division A (RoboCup), Champions (CBR)
milestones.description: Advances to 3rd place in Division A in the SSL category and wins the Brazilian Robotics Competition in the drone category.
